input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML — Use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current theme and picks `Light` or `Dark` automatically — no event wiring needed. Changes at runtime (user toggles system dark mode) are reflected instantly.

---

### C# alternative — `SetAppTheme<T>()`

The equivalent in code-behind or C# markup:

```csharp
// Extension method on any BindableObject
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,   // light value
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark value

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Get Started" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

### Key points

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Automatic** | Binds to `Application.RequestedTheme`; no manual subscription needed |
| **Runtime updates** | Reacts live when the user changes system theme |
| **Scope** | Works on any `BindableProperty` — colors, images, strings, etc. |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For app-wide consistency, define colors in `AppTheme`-aware resources in `App.xaml` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` |

For larger apps, prefer centralizing themed colors in `App.xaml`:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">
    <!-- won't work directly; use OnPlatform/AppThemeBinding in styles -->
</Color>

<!-- Better: use Styles -->
<Style TargetType="ContentPage">
    <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
            Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}" />
</Style>
```

This keeps individual pages clean and theme values in one place.
